While [Source 1] mentions that Tesla designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, energy generation and storage systems, and related products and services, and also offers vehicle service, charging infrastructure, and full self-driving capability software, it does not specify how Tesla organizes these activities into formal business segments for reporting purposes.

Tesla, Inc. 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Tesla, Inc. designs, develops, manufactures, leases, and sells electric vehicles, energy generation and storage systems, and related products and services. The Company also offers vehicle service, charging infrastructure, and full self-driving capability software. Tesla was incorporated in Delaware in July 2003.

Microsoft Corporation 10-K Annual Report — Fiscal Year 2024
United States Securities and Exchange Commission
Washington, D.C. 20549

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Microsoft Corporation enables digital transformation for the era of an intelligent cloud and an intelligent edge. Microsoft develops and supports a wide range of software products, services, devices, and solutions. The Company's fiscal year ends June 30.

Microsoft's segments are: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing.

e Company's business and financial condition are sensitive to global and regional macroeconomic conditions, including inflation, interest rates, and consumer spending trends. Uncertain or weak economic conditions may harm the Company's business and results of operations.

Competition Risk: The markets for the Company's products and services are highly competitive, and the Company is subject to intense competition in all areas of its business. Net income for fiscal 2024 was $93.7 billion, compared to $97.0 billion in fiscal 2023. Diluted earnings per share were $6.08, compared to $6.13 in fiscal 2023.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Fiscal Year 2024 Highlights

Total revenue for fiscal 2024 was $245.1 billion, compared to $211.9 billion in fiscal 2023, an increase of 16%. This growth was driven by strong performance in Intelligent Cloud and continued strength in Productivity and Business Processes.
